questions.question: Long exposure photography uses…
options: A slow shutter speed to capture movement over time
A very fast shutter speed to freeze motion
questions.reviewHint: Long exposures use slow shutter speeds so moving elements blur or smooth out.

questions.question: Which piece of gear is essential for sharp long exposures?
questions.reviewHint: A stable tripod is essential to keep static parts of the scene sharp during a long exposure.

questions.question: A remote shutter release (or self-timer) is used to…
options: Avoid camera shake from pressing the shutter
questions.reviewHint: Triggering without touching the camera prevents shake during the long exposure.

questions.question: A neutral density (ND) filter is used in long exposure to…
options: Reduce the light entering the lens so you can use a long shutter speed in bright conditions
questions.reviewHint: An ND filter cuts light, allowing long shutter speeds even in daylight.

questions.question: When an exposure needs to be longer than 30 seconds, you typically use…
questions.reviewHint: Bulb mode holds the shutter open beyond the usual 30-second limit for very long exposures.

questions.question: Long exposures of moving water typically produce…
options: A smooth, silky or misty effect
questions.reviewHint: Slow shutter speeds smooth flowing water into a silky, misty look.

questions.question: Light trails from traffic at night are created by…
options: A long exposure recording the moving lights as streaks
questions.reviewHint: A long exposure records moving car lights as continuous streaks.

questions.question: To keep noise low in long exposures, you should generally…
questions.reviewHint: Low/base ISO keeps long exposures clean; the tripod allows the slow shutter.

questions.question: A key first step before a long exposure is to…
options: Scout and compose the shot, then lock the camera down
questions.reviewHint: Compose carefully and stabilise before dialling in the long-exposure settings.

questions.question: Why focus and compose BEFORE attaching a strong ND filter?
options: A dark ND filter can make it hard for the camera to focus or for you to see the scene
ND filters change the focal length
Filters must always go on first
questions.reviewHint: Very dark NDs make focusing/composing hard, so set focus and framing first.

questions.question: Long-exposure noise reduction (dark-frame) is most useful for…
options: Very long exposures where sensor noise builds up
questions.reviewHint: Long exposures accumulate sensor noise; in-camera long-exposure NR helps clean it.
